rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND PANGBURN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 20, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Plaintiff¿s responses to contention interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ZIVA GVOZDENOVIC et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION, LTD.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 20, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Plant Products & Supply Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether the insulation to which Mr. Gvozdenovic was exposed by M. Slayen employees was sold, supplied or distributed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Slayen are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to decedent¿s declaration are overruled as to its admissibility as a dying declaration and sustained as lacking foundation and speculative only as to the statements that the insulation contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Cash¿s declaration are overruled as to its overall admissibility and sustained as lacking foundation and speculative only as to the statements that the insulation contained asbestos. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
